page: Microsoft Azure E4_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance divergence between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance sits in the average tier, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1409.99 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1226.0. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads show significant performance limitations. The multi-core stress-ng score of 3698.58 and Geekbench multi-core score of 2742.0 fall into the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds measuring 23.02 GB/sec and 18.0 GB/sec respectively, which are classified as weak.

From a resource density perspective, the E4_v4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense parallel processing. However, the tradeoff is a lower compute-to-memory value proposition, as evidenced by weak multi-threaded performance and poor multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 17.34 GB/sec. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachments, which may impact overall system throughput.

Given the high memory allocation paired with modest multi-core compute capabilities, this server is suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads. These include small-scale relational databases, caching layers, text processing pipelines, and basic web serving applications that benefit from a larger memory footprint but do not demand high-throughput parallel processing or local disk storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E4_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a distinct contrast: single-core capabilities are average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1226.0, whereas multi-core performance is weak, as shown by a multi-core stress-ng score of 3698.58. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block reads at 23.02 GB/sec. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive, compute-light workloads. It is best suited for caching layers, small databases, and text processing applications that require a larger memory footprint without demanding high parallel processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4_v4 is an Ev4-family VM with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL CPU. It offers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, featuring average single-core and weak multi-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads with moderate parallel computing demands.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB RAM
High memory density of 8.0 GB per core with no local storage
Average single-core performance contrasted by weak multi-core benchmark results
Constrained memory bandwidth with large-block reads at 23.02 GB/sec
Suited for caching layers, small databases, and memory-bound text processing
